figures.name: Imhotep
figures.significance: Architect of the Step Pyramid; first named architect in history; later deified

figures.name: Khufu (Cheops)
figures.significance: Commissioned the Great Pyramid of Giza

figures.name: Thutmose III
figures.significance: Greatest military pharaoh; expanded Egypt to its greatest territorial extent

figures.name: Akhenaten
figures.significance: Revolutionary monotheist attempt (Aten worship); rejected by successors

figures.name: Ramesses II
figures.significance: Signed the first known peace treaty in history (with the Hittites)

name: Ptolemaic Kingdom
note: Greek-speaking Macedonian rulers adopted Egyptian royal titles and iconography for legitimacy, but the court was Hellenic

figures.name: Ptolemy I Soter
figures.significance: Macedonian general who founded the dynasty; established the Library of Alexandria

figures.name: Cleopatra VII
figures.significance: Last active Ptolemaic ruler; only one to learn Egyptian; her death ended Greek rule in Egypt

name: Kingdom of Meroe (Meroitic Kingdom)
note: After the Kushite 25th Dynasty was expelled from Egypt by the Assyrians (-656), Nubian power retreated south to Meroe on the Nile. Meroe developed a distinct civilisation with its own undeciphered script (Meroitic), iron smelting industry, and distinctive pyramid style (steeper than Egyptian). Ruled by queen-regents (Kandakes) alongside or instead of kings. Fell to the Aksumite kingdom of Ethiopia around 350 CE.

figures.name: Taharqa
figures.significance: Last great Kushite-Egyptian pharaoh; mentioned in the Bible (2 Kings 19) as an ally against Assyria; built extensively at Karnak; expelled from Egypt by Assyrians but continued to rule Nubia

figures.name: Amanirenas (Kandake)
figures.significance: Blind queen who led Meroitic forces against the Roman occupation of Egypt (24 BCE); raided Aswan and carried off a bronze head of Augustus; negotiated a favourable peace treaty with Rome — one of the few rulers to successfully resist Roman expansion
